_comment: Aggregated MATHIR test results (V7.5.0 - V8.0.0). Includes BEIR benchmarks, vision model tests, and vector search comparisons.
sources: benchmark_unified.py (numpy vs FAISS vs USearch vs sqlite-vec)

beir_sciFact.notes: MATHIR reference point. BM25 baseline 0.5438, hybrid RRF 0.6602, hybrid+CE 0.5910 (BM25 dilutes dense, CE is wrong domain).

beir_nfcorpus.notes: Medical terminology with Latin taxonomic names preserved through NFKD normalization. FTS5 handles abbreviations (DNA, MRI, PCR).

beir_arguana.notes: Immunological tier caught contradictory arguments and flagged them. Working tier preserved debate context. Resolved V7.5.0 'pending' state.

episodic_recovery.notes: LIRS (Low Inter-reference Recency Set) replaces naive FIFO. After 30 min warmup, hit 100% on temporal queries.

immunological_cold_start.notes: Ensemble of Mahalanobis + cdist threshold + novelty detector. Fixed threshold prevents cold-start collapse.

working_memory_isolation.notes: Working tier is intentionally lossy (88-90% is the design target). Perfect recall lives in episodic/semantic. Zero cross-thread contamination.

kl_router_accuracy.notes: Supervised training + KL constraint. Entropy stayed below collapse (0.61 vs prior 1.33/1.39). 25% random baseline -> 100% correct tier routing.

integration_stress_test.mixed_workload: all 4 tiers + cross-model recall + mid-session model switch
integration_stress_test.notes: 50 memories stored with vision model, 50/50 recalled with text-only model. Mid-session model switch with zero memory loss. FTS5 index stayed consistent.

universal_bridge_tests.notes: Store with Provider A, recall with Provider B. 78 built-in abbreviations (DNA=deoxyribonucleic acid, MRI, PCR). Diacritics (Schrödinger<->Schrodinger). Roman numerals (Henry VIII<->Henry 8 = 1.0 score). Compound terms (sternocleidomastoid->[sterno, cleido, mastoid]).

ollama_models.notes: Three different architectures (Qwen, Liquid LFM, IBM Granite) all successfully retrieved correct memories from MATHIR shared db. qwen3.5:2b exceeded inference budget.

openrouter_free_models.notes: Survey pass - goal was to identify which free-tier models are viable. 4/27 working is the empirical result, not a failure of the survey.

universal_recall_demo.notes: MATHIRMemory.universal_recall() new API. 'What do you know about python closures?' works, 'clotures python' (FR) works, MiniMax provider with no stored embeddings falls back to primary, hybrid text+embedding query works. list_providers() helper returned empty - non-blocking.

summary.partial_detail: ollama_models (3/4 architectures passed; qwen3.5:2b timed out)

vector_search_benchmarks._comment: BEIR SciFact (5183 docs, 1109 queries) + vector backend comparison. RTX 4060, bge-large-en-v1.5 1024d CUDA.

faiss_vs_numpy.conclusion: numpy 1.5x faster than FAISS at MATHIR scale

accuracy_tests._comment: Per-model vision accuracy battery. Populated by accuracy_tests.AccuracyTestFramework. Schema: {model_name: {last_run, overall_score, tests_passed, tests_run, by_type, by_image, per_test[]}}. See vision_testing/accuracy_tests.py and accuracy_benchmarks/results/<model>_<timestamp>.json for the full untruncated responses.

_test_battery.scoring: 0.0-1.0 float per test; 0.7+ counts as PASS. Object detection, color recognition, and counting use keyword/partial-credit matching. Person detection is a weighted blend of presence/count/clothing. Grounding uses IoU + tolerance-px banding.
